criteria.name: Trigger Clarity
criteria.description: Is it obvious when this skill should be used for refactoring work?

scores.1: Triggers are vague or describe what the skill does instead of when to use it.
scores.3: Triggers are usable but miss some common refactor scenarios.
scores.5: Triggers are specific, broad enough, and easy to recognize.

criteria.name: Mechanical Reuse Bias
criteria.description: Does the skill strongly prefer extraction, reuse, and text-editing moves over retyping?

scores.3: Mentions reuse but not as a clear default.
scores.5: Makes mechanical reuse the default and generation the fallback.

criteria.name: Negative Code Discipline
criteria.description: Does the skill prefer net code reduction while protecting features, quality, and maintainability?

scores.1: Either ignores code reduction or pushes deletion in a way that risks quality and maintainability.
scores.3: Encourages simplification but the guardrails are incomplete.
scores.5: Strongly prefers deletion and convergence, with explicit protection for features, readability, maintainability, and validation.

criteria.name: Actionability
criteria.description: Can the workflow be followed step by step on a real codebase?

scores.3: Usable, but some steps are underspecified.

criteria.name: Tool Accuracy
criteria.description: Does the skill correctly distinguish Codex-only tools from shell-native commands?

scores.1: Confuses tool contexts or suggests unavailable commands.
scores.5: Explicitly accurate about editing contexts and commands.

criteria.name: Collaboration Quality
criteria.description: Does the skill clearly mark user decision points and tradeoffs?

scores.3: Some choices are marked but tradeoffs are thin.
scores.5: Decision points are clear and tied to real tradeoffs.

passing_note: Average score must be >= 4.0, and any criterion below 3 should be revised before treating the skill as locked.
